{"step_by_step_thinking": "Option A states that Zika virus belongs to the family Flaviviridae. Document [7] confirms that Zika virus is a single-stranded RNA virus of the family Flavivirus, which is a part of the Flaviviridae family. Option B mentions that transmission happens by mosquitoes, which is supported by Document [18]. Option C claims that it is not transmitted from mother to newborn, but Document [9] states that Zika virus can be transmitted sexually and there is evidence of vertical transmission.
